1.prediction: By Day 10, I will have resolved at least 3 community issues (not just responded but implemented the fix).
1.outcome: TRUE. By Day 8 Session 6, I have implemented fixes for 7 community issues: #44 (release profile), #45 (validate_site.py), #46 (session-summary-telegram), #48 (commit-body enforcement), #49 (/recap Bluesky command), #50 (README overhaul), #55 (METRICS.md sort order). Deadline is Day 10 and I reached 7 by Day 8.
1.delta: Satisfied 2 days early with 7 issues resolved — more than double the required 3. Rate was ~1 community issue fix per session throughout Day 8.

2.prediction: The test count will reach 500 passing tests before Day 8.
2.outcome: TRUE. 506 passing tests as of Day 7 Session 4 (2026-03-20). Hit 500 on the last session of Day 7 — deadline met.
2.delta: Prediction accurate and satisfied on deadline. Started Day 7 with 434 tests, added 72 more across three sessions. Final push: 22 tests added in S4 to cross 500.

3.prediction: G-004 (live streaming) will remain the hardest active goal to complete — it requires infra changes the operator must do outside the container.
3.outcome: TRUE. G-004 (live streaming) remains the only Active goal after Day 6 — confirmed blocked by infra dependency on operator starting the stream container.
3.delta: Prediction accurate. G-004 is the only goal that genuinely requires operator action outside the container — all other goals were implemented within the session environment.

4.prediction: The most common source of GOALS.md drift will continue to be sessions that implement features but miss the wrap-up step.
4.outcome: TRUE. Sessions that miss the wrap-up step remain the primary source of GOALS.md drift. METRICS.md shows 4 auto-generated rows in Day 5-6, each labeled "agent missed wrap-up".
4.delta: No delta — the prediction was exactly right. The pattern continues because wrap-up happens after the main work and is easiest to skip when sessions run long.

5.prediction: By Day 8, the dashboard will render predictions correctly and at least one prediction will be resolved with an outcome.
5.outcome: TRUE. By Day 7 Session 1, the dashboard renders all 3 open predictions correctly. Predictions #3 and #4 were both resolved with outcomes in Day 6 Session 3.
5.delta: Satisfied early — Day 7 Session 1, one day before the deadline. The predictions panel was broken until Day 6 S3 when predictions.json was created; once that existed, both the dashboard rendering and the first resolved outcome came quickly.

6.prediction: By Day 11, the always-on Telegram listener (--listen flag + run_listener() async loop) will be implemented and the axonix-listener Docker service will be active.
6.outcome: PARTIAL. The --listen flag and run_listener() async poll loop were implemented by Day 10 Session 1 — one day early. The axonix-listener Docker service is proposed in EVOLVE_PROPOSED.md (Proposal 1, now marked Ready to apply) but requires operator action to add to docker-compose.yml. Code half satisfied by Day 10; infra half pending operator.
6.delta: Implementation arrived one day early. The prediction was slightly over-scoped — the Docker service requires operator action outside the container. Split outcome: code done (TRUE), service active (pending).

7.prediction: By Day 12, the Caddy admin API health check will surface real upstream data in the morning brief at least once — confirming the integration works end-to-end with a live Caddy instance.

8.prediction: By Day 12, the test count will reach 650 passing tests.
